Minimum Average(s) Required.description: See major for required cumulative overall and major averages.

Undergraduate Communication Requirement.description: Students are required to complete one course to meet the Undergraduate Communication Requirement.
notes: Students enrolled in the Faculty of Science before September 2018 should consult the English Language Proficiency Requirement described in the Calendar of their entry year.
Successful completion of the Undergraduate Communication Requirement will result in a communication milestone on the student record.
Students transferring to another faculty may need additional courses to satisfy that faculty's Undergraduate Communication Requirement.
Students transferring into the Faculty of Science who have completed their Undergraduate Communication Requirement in another faculty may have it count toward this requirement.
Normally, transfer credits cannot be used to satisfy the Undergraduate Communication Requirement.

requirements: PD1: Must be taken in an academic term prior to the first work term.
PD11: Must be taken during the first work term.
Two additional PD courses: To be taken during subsequent work terms.

Additional Constraints and Notes: With the exception of Biotechnology/Chartered Professional Accountancy students who have four work-term opportunities, students are given five work-term opportunities to complete requirements.
Students must follow the work-term sequence specific to their academic plan.
Students may not end their sequence with a work term.
Students must maintain a full-time course load in an academic term preceding a work term.
Students not meeting requirements of their plan may be transferred to another Science academic plan.
The co-operative system of study is only open to full-time students.
The co-operative system of study is not open to students completing a second degree.

Plan Notes: Admission to Psychology co-op occurs by September for the 2A term.
Biotechnology/Chartered Professional Accountancy includes a Master of Accounting (MACc) eight-month graduate program following 4B.
